feedback.details: Mermaid diagram (lines 51-74) is syntactically valid C4Context format. Uses correct element types (Person_Ext, System, Container_Ext), proper relationship arrows (->, <->), and valid UpdateLayoutConfig at line 73. No syntax errors or warnings expected.

Diagram contains exactly required elements: 1 System (LlmSwitch, line 58), 2 Persons (Developer line 55, Operations line 56), and 4 Container_Ext elements (Prometheus line 60, Consul line 61, Vault line 62, Orchestration line 63) matching PRD section 'Integrations' [PRD 385-392]. No additional or missing elements.

Every diagram element cites PRD sections: Developer/Operations [PRD 5], LlmSwitch [PRD 1], external dependencies [PRD 10.1]. Element-to-PRD mapping table (lines 87-97) provides complete bidirectional traceability. Correctly excludes OpenAPI/Anthropic as external systems per explanatory note (lines 26-33).

Narrative exceeds 200 words (approx. 500+ words). Contains required element mapping table (lines 87-97). Dedicated paragraph (lines 35-47) explains why each dependency is necessary: Prometheus for observability, Consul for service discovery, Vault for security compliance, Orchestration for deployment management.

Relationship table (lines 78-85) specifies Direction, Protocol, and Authentication for all relationships. Minor issue: Line 71 'LlmSwitch -> Orchestration : Deployed as job via API' has slightly ambiguous semantics - the label suggests passive deployment by Orchestration, but arrow direction shows LlmSwitch initiating communication. Could be clarified to 'Reports status to' or similar, or arrow direction adjusted to Orchestration -> LlmSwitch for deployment relationship.

No hallucinated external dependencies. Diagram contains exactly the 4 external containers listed in PRD 'Integrations': Prometheus, Consul, Vault, Orchestration. Explicit note at lines 26-33 justifies exclusion of OpenAPI/Anthropic as API specifications rather than external systems. Zero extra containers present.

System boundary correctly encloses only LlmSwitch (line 58). All external dependencies are Container_Ext elements (lines 60-63) placed outside the system boundary. No external container incorrectly drawn inside boundary, and LlmSwitch is correctly positioned as the System element.

C4 naming conventions correctly applied: Persons use Sentence case (Developer, Operations), Containers use PascalCase (LlmSwitch, Prometheus, Consul, Vault, Orchestration). Relationship labels use sentence case and all are <=25 characters: 'Sends LLM requests' (17), 'Deploys & manages system' (23), 'Exports metrics via HTTP' (22), 'DNS/HTTP service discovery' (25), 'Gets secrets via AppRole' (22), 'Deployed as job via API' (23).

File passes requirements: Frontmatter present (lines 1-4) with sprint_number and sprint_name. No tabs (spaces only). No inline HTML. Line lengths appear to comply with MD013 (<=120 chars). Minor: Consider adding explicit markdownlint configuration or verify line 36 ('Each external dependency from PRD 'Integrations' is essential:') and similar long lines are within limits.

overall_summary: c1-context.md is a production-ready architecture document that fully satisfies all sprint contract criteria. The Mermaid C4Context diagram correctly implements Level 1 requirements with proper element counts (1 System, 2 Persons, 4 Container_Ext). PRD traceability is excellent with bidirectional mapping between diagram elements and PRD sections. The narrative provides substantial depth with required word count, element mapping table, and detailed rationale for each external dependency. Relationship specifications are complete with protocols, directions, and authentication methods, with only minor ambiguity on the Orchestration relationship label. No hallucinated dependencies exist, and boundary integrity is maintained. C4 naming conventions are correctly applied. Version control requirements are met with proper formatting. This is an exceptional implementation suitable for production deployment.
